channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New Champion Locke Revealed in Latest League of Legends Cinematic
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released a new 2D-animated cinematic showcasing League of Legends’ new champion, Locke.

The ‘Back from the Brink’ cinematic reveals Locke as some sort of demon hunter, fitting perfectly into this season’s theme.

Locke will be the 173rd playable champion in the MOBA, following on from Zaahen who released back in November last year.

Riot Games has already confirmed that Locke will be the only champion released in League of Legends in 2026.

This is so that Riot could focus on VGUs for older champions like Shyvana and one other Demacian character, as well as preparing for the League Next update, which will overhaul the game in 2027.

Locke is a new Demacian champion, nicknamed the Ashen Exorcist by Riot, who is rumoured to be an AP Assassin.

The cinematic reveals him to be a blonde man who carries a coffin on his back full of giant nails, which he uses to fight.

The description of the video reads: “Locke has hunted demons his whole life, but a possessed Vayne will be his greatest challenge yet.”

Throughout the video, he fights against Vayne, who has been taken over by demonic spirits, just like her new Demoncursed Vayne skin.

Fighting Vayne one-on-one, Locke throws a skull onto the ground, which seems to swallow everything in sight, including the demons Vayne has been afflicted with.

Locke then reaches out to Vayne’s own consciousness and frees her to her usual self.

What is Locke and Vayne’s Relationship?

Riot Games has not confirmed any more of Locke’s lore, but it’s clear from the cinematic that he has some kind of connection to Vayne.

While trying to save her mid-fight, Locke refers to her by her real name, Shauna.

And once free, Vayne also recognises Locke and then tells him: “This doesn’t mean we’re even,” which suggests that perhaps she had previously saved him.

When Does Locke Release in LoL?

Riot Games has confirmed that Locke will release on Wednesday, June 24.

This will coincide with the release of LoL Patch 26.13, the same patch that the esports tournament MSI will be played on.

However, it’s unlikely that the new champion will be enabled for the pro-play event.

What are your first impressions of Locke? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, Senna and Thresh have been confirmed for LoL-based fighter 2XKO.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Has a ‘Singular Ending’
item.content:encoded:
The Final Fantasy 7 Remake series director revealed that Revelation features choices but has a ‘singular ending’.

After years of anticipation, Square Enix has finally revealed the next installment of the Final Fantasy 7 Remake trilogy. They also provided players with a release window for all platforms, along with some new gameplay footage. However, there is still much more to learn about the title before its release, and some of these details have been shared in a recent interview.

Final Fantasy 7 Revelation’s Journey Might Differ, But Ending Will be the Same for Everyone

In a new interview with GamesRadar+, Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director Naoki Hamaguchi discussed the game’s singular ending despite its choices. Hamaguchi first discussed how they do not wish to have a “singular storytelling experience for every single player around the world.” Instead, they wish to provide a unique experience for each player joining them for the finale.

Hamaguchi explains that a player’s choices in the game will affect specific moments when each character faces their final battle. The story will reflect their resolve as they head into the “final battle that determines the fate of the planet”. The goal of these choices is to change the approach and depth of these moments for the characters in the final chapter.

At the same time, the director understands that there will be “a lot of attention” on how the final chapter ends and how everything plays out. No details on the ending were revealed, but it was confirmed that Final Fantasy 7 Revelation will have a “singular ending.” Which means that how you get to the ending might differ, but all roads will eventually lead to the same end.

In other news, Final Fantasy 11 developers are researching ways to bypass technical limits that currently block new areas and future expansion. Additionally, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3’s director has confirmed that they have left the door open for DLC. What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Revelation having a singular ending? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Nintendo Direct being confirmed for June 9. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Strands #827 Hints and Answers for Today, June 8, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #827 for Monday, June 8, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 8, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #826 are all terms related to reptiles.

General Strands Hints for June 8

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Poker or potato.
  • Hint 2: Essential for playing Dungeons & Dragons.
  • Hint 3: Nintendo got its start manufacturing these.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #827 for June 8, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 8, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • PINE
  • PINS
  • GRAD
  • GRADE
  • LAST

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #827 on June 8, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • CHIPS
  • DICE
  • CARDS
  • BOARDS
  • TILES
  • TOKENS
  • SPINNERS
  • SPANAGRAM: GAMENIGHT

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #827?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 9 will be Strands Game #828.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All Confirmed & Rumored Games For June 9 Nintendo Direct
item.content:encoded:
The showcase season is beginning to come to a close, with most of the heavy hitters having had their time in the spotlight. However, there’s one big name left, and that’s Nintendo, with a Direct now confirmed for June 9 at 7 AM PT.

So far, it’s generally been an incredible Summer for gamers, with a wealth of new announcements across the board. That said, the fun isn’t over yet, as Nintendo are set to round it all out—but what games can we expect to see during the Direct?

It’s a valid question, as Nintendo have been as elusive as ever, but we’ve put together a list of what you can expect to see during the June 9 Nintendo Direct—check them all out below:

Every Confirmed Game For The Nintendo Direct

As things stand, no games have been confirmed to appear at the June 9 Nintendo Direct, though there are a few safe bets.

The obvious title that should appear during the June 9 Nintendo Direct is Star Fox, the remake of the classic Nintendo 64 game. With a release date of June 25, it’s likely a new trailer will appear to get fans hyped one last time before the game’s back in our hands.

  • More Insider Gaming: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

As for other games, we’ll probably see more of Splatoon Raiders and Fire Emblem Shadows, with both games due to be released in the coming months. As for everything else? That’s where the speculating begins…

Every Rumored Game For The Nintendo Direct

There’s room for plenty of surprises at this Nintendo Direct, whether it be new ports of beloved titles or entirely new announcements. Here’s what we think has a strong chance of appearing based on rumors and speculation.

Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Remake

This will most likely be one of the heavy hitters for the showcase, and it’s long been rumored that it’s in development. With a supposed release window of Christmas this year, an official reveal would go down a treat.

3D Mario

Mario has been killing it at the box office lately, but he’s long overdue for a return to the 3D gaming space. According to reports, the next 3D Mario does exist, but it’s not due until 2027. That said, could we still see it announced this year, with gameplay to follow in the future? Fans wishing for Mario Odyssey 2 would certainly hope so.

New Switch Sports Title

The Wii Sports era will forever remain unmatched, but there are rumors circulating that a new Switch Sports title is in the works. The last one was a fine outing, if not slightly underwhelming, but with the capabilities of the Switch 2, it feels like it’s only a matter of time before a comprehensive sports title takes off again.

The Duskbloods

The Duskbloods is a Nintendo exclusive FromSoftware game that has plenty of fans excited, though the release date for the 2026-slated game still remains unknown. Could we get a better glimpse of the game during the Direct, as well as an idea of when we can finally get our hands on it? Fingers crossed!

Mario Kart World DLC

Mario Kart World has been out for roughly a year now, and that means it’s high time for some DLC. Whether that would take the form of new additions to its character roster or courses remains to be seen, but fans of the racer would undoubtedly take both.

Pokémon Pokopia DLC

Sticking with the DLC theme, there’s no denying that Pokopia was a smash hit, and many fans have been calling for new explorable areas where they can gather even more Pokémon. It feels like the possibilities are endless with this one, and it’s bound to happen at some point, so why not now?

Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave

The next instalment in the Fire Emblem franchise is slated for a 2026 release window, though little has been heard of the game since its announcement. It’s well overdue for an update, with a potential release date on the cards.

A Ton Of Switch 2 Third-Party Ports

The Nintendo Switch 2 collects ports like Pokémon, and throughout Summer Game Fest, a host of games have already been confirmed for the platform. Well, it likely won’t stop there, as some of the best games from the past few years are bound to arrive on the handheld, with a ton already leaked or rumored, including:

  • Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
  • Monster Hunter Wilds
  • Metaphor: ReFantazio
  • Mewgenics
  • Guardians of the Galaxy
  • Starfield
  • Stellar Blade

And so many more! It’s like shooting fish in a barrel trying to guess which ones could appear, so go in with an open mind!

Other Potential Appearances

As usual, there are bound to be plenty of surprises during the Nintendo Direct, and it’s impossible to predict them all. That said, here are a few more titles that could appear at the event, even if they’re along the lines of wishful thinking:

  • New Zelda title
  • Something Kirby (Remake?)
  • New WarioLand/WarioWare
  • Super Metroid Remake
  • Metroid 6
  • Rhythm Heaven Groove
  • New Xenoblade Chronicles

Are you excited for the Nintendo Direct? Do you think we’re missing any potential reveals? If so, let us know in the comments below, or share your pre-show predictions in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced
  • XBOX Games Showcase 2026—Everything Announced
  • Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
